arguments.title: FY2024 Full-Year Results — Financial Summary
arguments.body:
FY2024 Results: A Clear View of Our Performance

For fiscal year 2024, Apple delivered a disciplined performance across products and services. The company reported revenue of $391.0 billion, reflecting steady demand across key product lines and continued momentum in Services. Our reported net income was $93.7 billion with operating income of $123.2 billion, underlining the durability of our business model.

Key financial highlights

  • Revenue: $391.0 billion for FY2024.
  • Services revenue: approximately $96 billion, a meaningful contributor to recurring revenue.
  • Gross margin: 46.2% for the year.
  • EPS (diluted): $6.11.
  • Share repurchases: $95 billion returned to shareholders in FY2024.
  • Balance sheet: cash & securities of $153.0 billion and total debt of $101.3 billion.

What this means for employees

These results reflect the collective work of our teams worldwide — from hardware engineering to services, from retail Specialists to supply chain partners. The growth of Services (nearly $96 billion) reinforces our strategy to build long-term recurring value alongside exceptional products like iPhone, Mac, and Vision Pro.

“We continue to invest in the future while returning capital to shareholders.” — Executive summary

Segment context

iPhone remains our largest segment by revenue, supported by the recent product cycles. Mac and iPad continue to serve creative and professional customers, while Wearables, Home & Accessories and Services contribute diversified revenue streams. These results give us room to invest in R&D, particularly in Apple Silicon, spatial computing, and privacy-preserving AI.

Looking ahead

As we enter the next fiscal year, the company will focus on product excellence, expanding Services, and delivering on our long-term environmental and social commitments. Managers should reference the Finance & Investor page for detailed quarter-by-quarter breakdowns and communications guidance.

Further reading: Read the full report and investor materials at investor.apple.com.

arguments.title: iPhone 16 Family — Internal Launch Recap
arguments.body:
iPhone 16: Launch Recap for Employees

The iPhone 16 family introduces key hardware and software refinements, including the new Camera Control feature and Apple’s A18 chip improvements. This launch continues the product cadence that drives the company’s largest revenue segment.

Launch highlights

  • New camera controls and imaging features designed to improve photo and video capture across all models.
  • Performance uplift with the A18 chip for the Pro models, improving efficiency and computational photography.
  • Retail and online launch coordination across our 516 stores globally and Apple.com.

Retail & support readiness

Retail teams should reference the Retail & Stores launch playbook for staffing, Genius Bar expectations, and customer experience scripts. Product Innovation has provided technical notes for troubleshooting common issues.

Engineering notes

Engineering teams may review the Product Innovation channel for teardown findings, test results, and firmware updates relevant to device servicing and diagnostics.

CTA: Specialists and Geniuses can find checklists on the Retail & Stores channel and diagnostic guides on Product Innovation.

arguments.title: Vision Pro — First-Year Review & adoption highlights
arguments.body:
Vision Pro: First-Year Review

One year since launch, Vision Pro has established a clear foothold in spatial computing. Adoption among developers and early enterprise partners shows promise for immersive experiences, while the hardware and developer ecosystem continue to mature.

Adoption & developer interest

  • Growing developer interest in spatial apps and productivity use-cases.
  • Active developer resources for porting existing iPad and macOS experiences to visionOS are available on the Product Innovation channel.

Product direction

Vision Pro’s unique UI paradigms drive investments in human-interface guidelines and accessibility. Teams should consult Product Innovation for best practices and to request test hardware when needed.

Further reading: For developer guidance and submission notes, see the VisionOS documentation on Product Innovation.

arguments.title: Services Growth — Subscriptions & Trends
arguments.body:
Services Momentum: Subscriptions & Trends

Services continued to be a strong growth engine. Subscriptions across App Store, iCloud, Apple Music, Apple TV+, and AppleCare bundle recurring revenue while improving customer lifetime value.

Numbers to know

  • Services revenue for FY2024 was approximately $96 billion.
  • Growth is driven by both international expansion and bundled offerings.

Employee implications

Cross-functional teams should align on services messaging during launches to highlight subscription benefits and support offers. Marketing, retail, and product teams have co-owned content to educate customers and internal teams.

For more: Services product managers can find campaign toolkits on the Product Innovation and Apple Newsroom channels.

arguments.title: CEO Quarterly Letter — Q4 Reflections
arguments.body:
A Message from the CEO — Q4 Reflections

As we close the quarter, I want to recognise the dedication of our teams around the world. This quarter reflected strong execution across product launches and strategic investments, and I'm proud of the focus on innovation and customer privacy that underpins our work.

Leadership priorities

  • Maintaining our commitment to user privacy and security.
  • Investing in Apple Silicon and next-generation compute for product differentiation.
  • Growing Services responsibly while expanding into new markets.

Closing: Thank you for your leadership and continued commitment to excellence. — CEO

arguments.title: Board Meeting Summary — June
arguments.body:
Board Meeting Highlights — June

The Board reviewed progress on strategic initiatives including AI investments, supply chain resilience, and the new privacy initiatives. Key action items included enhanced oversight of AI risk and continued investment in R&D.

  • Approval to expand R&D allocations for advanced silicon.
  • Directives to strengthen compliance reporting across regions.
  • Updates on shareholder engagement and governance best practices.

Executives should brief their teams on the board directives relevant to their roadmap priorities.

,
